holdings.name: HDFC Bank
holdings.description: Largest Sensex constituent. India's most profitable private bank. Post-merger with HDFC Ltd, now India's largest home loan lender too. 8,000+ branches, stable NIMs and consistently best-in-class asset quality.

holdings.name: Reliance Industries
holdings.description: India's largest company and 2nd largest Sensex constituent. Jio's digital dominance, Retail's store expansion and O2C cash flows fund a massive new energy transition — solar manufacturing, green H2 and EV battery factories.

holdings.name: ICICI Bank
holdings.description: Sensex's 3rd largest holding. Highest ROE among large private banks at 18%+. 5-star rated digital app, zero-slippage retail portfolio, strong overseas business. Key beneficiary of India credit upcycle.

holdings.name: Infosys
holdings.description: 4th largest Sensex stock. Leads in gen-AI-driven outsourcing with Infosys Topaz. Board-approved buybacks support EPS growth. 30 consecutive years of dividend payment. Preferred partner for banking and insurance digital transformation.

holdings.name: L&T
holdings.description: India's infrastructure backbone. Sensex's 5th largest. Order inflows accelerating with India's NIP spending. Defence manufacturing (warships, missiles, radars) adds a high-margin, multi-year revenue stream.

holdings.name: Tata Consultancy Services
holdings.description: India's IT bellwether and a core Sensex holding. $4B+ quarterly revenue; 95%+ client retention. BFSI, Retail, Life Sciences verticals anchoring growth. Returning capital via buybacks at scale.
